reasons: Codex and openclaw form a convergent cluster with similar results (251 vs 209 stocks, 18% vs 15%)
Both high-count agents independently calculated ~15-18% of ChiNext stocks meet the <5% Bollinger width threshold
This proportion is more plausible than the extremely low results from ga (0.45%) and claudecode (0.15%)
All four agents satisfy the output contract (correct file format, valid numbers, consistency check passed)
Codex shows slightly higher count (251 vs 209), suggesting more comprehensive data coverage or less aggressive filtering
The 42-stock difference between codex and openclaw is relatively small compared to the 125x variance across all agents
Given the explicit 5% threshold in the task, a ~15-18% detection rate is reasonable for Bollinger squeeze conditions
merge_notes: All agents produced valid outputs meeting the contract requirements. The key differentiator is the actual count result. Two distinct clusters emerged: low-count (2-6 stocks) and high-count (209-251 stocks). The high-count cluster shows better convergence and more plausible results for a 5% bandwidth threshold. Codex is preferred over openclaw by a small margin due to slightly higher coverage, though both are acceptable solutions. The low-count results from ga and claudecode appear to be overly strict interpretations or calculation errors.
